codeReview.summary: The feature mostly matches requirements (prompt update + post_process integration), but the integration introduces a correctness gap: IDs are deduplicated before post_process, while post_process can normalize IDs to the same value, allowing duplicate IDs to be returned.

issues.description: Deduplication occurs before calling post_process (see line 262), but post_process normalizes IDs (extract/post_processor.py:206, 213). This can create duplicate IDs after return (e.g., 'asce7-22-26.5-P1' and 'ASCE7-22-26.5-P1' both normalize to 'ASCE7-22-26.5-P1'). Reproduced with a local Python snippet: output IDs became duplicated. Re-deduplicate after post_process (or normalize IDs before dedupe) to preserve unique-ID invariant.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared guidance does not currently document a rule for transform ordering when ID-mutating post-processors are introduced (normalize IDs before/with final dedupe).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md and .factory/library/architecture.md describe post-processing and dedupe behavior at a high level, but no explicit invariant/procedure prevents duplicate IDs introduced by ID normalization after dedupe; implementation at extract/llm_structurer.py:262-267 shows this gap.

summary: Reviewed transcript skeleton and commit 951acde. Prompt/type integration is present, but a blocking duplicate-ID edge case exists due to dedupe-before-normalization ordering, so this review fails pending a follow-up fix.
